items.text:
**Problem:** UI renders `unknown_event` entries (with `× 2` dedupe badges) instead of properly handling unrecognized event types in the transcript stream.
**Expected:** Unknown events are either rendered with a meaningful fallback view or filtered/logged gracefully.
**Actual:** Raw `unknown_event` rows appear next to real entries (e.g., a Bash `scp ... && ssh ...` call), leaking internal event-type names to the user.
**Context:** Repro payload begins `{"ts":"2026-05-21T20...`; timestamps `04:00:10`; surfaces in the Bash tool-call transcript view.

comments.text:
**Fixed.** `unknown_event` rows no longer leak into the chat pane.

### Root cause

`agent-session.js:669` wraps any SDK message type myco doesn't recognize as `_emit({ type: 'unknown_event', raw_type: m.type, raw: m })` — server-side passthrough so phase-2+ code can decide how to handle them, and so events.jsonl preserves the diagnostic record. Pre-fix, the client's render path fell through to:
- `_chromeShortLabel` line 5088: `return ev.type || 'event'` → "unknown_event" literal in chrome batch head
- `_chromeEventLine` line 4540: `let kind = ev.type || 'event'` + JSON dump in expanded body

Two adjacent `unknown_event` events would fold via the chrome batch (`unknown_event` was in `AGENT_CHROME_TYPES`), producing the `× 2 unknown_event` row the user reported.

### Fix — surgical, two edits in app.js

1. **Short-circuit at top of `_appendAgentEvent`** — `if (ev.type === 'unknown_event') { console.warn('[unknown_event]', ev.raw_type, ev.raw); return; }`. Fires BEFORE chrome routing so the event never lands in the DOM.
2. **Removed `'unknown_event'`** from `AGENT_CHROME_TYPES` (consistency — the short-circuit makes the listing dead code). Trail-marker comment in the Set explains the omission to prevent a future "tidy" re-adding it.

### What's preserved

- **Server still emits** `unknown_event` → events.jsonl still records the diagnostic
- **DevTools `console.warn`** with `[unknown_event]` marker keeps it visible to devs without polluting the user UI

### Test

`test/bug-25-unknown-event-suppress.test.js` — **7 cases**:
- server origin still emits unknown_event (don't break diagnostics)
- _appendAgentEvent has the short-circuit at the top (before chrome routing)
- short-circuit returns (no fall-through)
- console.warn surfaces it to devs
- AGENT_CHROME_TYPES no longer lists unknown_event
- trail-marker comment present
- _chromeShortLabel still has the `ev.type || 'event'` fallback (documents WHY we short-circuit)

Wired into test.sh. Per CLAUDE.md pre-commit §1: full sweep — 61/61 individual test files green.

items.text:
**Problem:** No way to narrow plan items by type or quickly find one by name in the view.
**Expected:** A filter by type control at the top of the view, plus a fuzz search for plan items.
**Actual:** View shows all plan items with no type filter or search affordance.

comments.text:
**Done — type filter + fuzz search shipped on the Plan tab.**

### What landed

Second row under the Plan header — three pill-shaped chips + a search input:

```
[Plan]                              [☐ Open only] [↻ Refresh]
[🐞 Bug] [✨ Feature] [✅ Todo]   [🔍 Search plan items…              ]
```

### Behavior

| Filter | Source | Persisted? | Default |
|---|---|---|---|
| Open only (bug-15, pre-existing) | localStorage.`myco_plan_open_only` | yes | OFF |
| Type chips (Bug / Feature / Todo) | localStorage.`myco_plan_type_filter` | yes | ALL ENABLED |
| Search (substring, case-insensitive) | `state.planSearchQuery` | **no** (fresh each reload) | empty |

All three intersect (AND). Search hits item `id` + `text` + `body`. 150ms debounce — fast feel without thrashing the renderer.

### Why these specific defaults
- Type chips default-on so the change is invisible to users who don't engage with it (matches the bug-15 opt-in pattern).
- Search not-persisted because reloading into a stale query is hostile; the user is unlikely to want yesterday's typed query waiting for them. Type filters DO persist because they're a deliberate scope choice the user expects to stick.

### Implementation shape
- `web/public/index.html` — added `#plan-filter-row` subheader with the chips + `#plan-search` input.
- `web/public/styles.css` — `.artifact-subheader`, `.plan-type-chip` (uses `:has(input:checked)` for active state, no JS sync needed), `.plan-search-input`, plus a mobile breakpoint that gives the search input its own line on ≤600px.
- `web/public/app.js` — added `_filterPlanItems(items, {openOnly, types, search})` as a TOP-LEVEL function (so the test can mirror its semantics), plus `_readPlanTypeFilter()`, `bindPlanTypeFilters()`, `bindPlanSearch()`. runs.result:
**bug-26 fixed** as `c907d62`.

**Behavior**:
- User at bottom (within 50px) → new messages still pin to latest, as before
- User scrolled up to read earlier messages → new messages **leave them in place**
- User scrolls back near the bottom → next message resumes auto-scroll naturally

- `CHAT_SCROLL_BOTTOM_THRESHOLD = 50` (standard browser-affordance cutoff)

**Caller-intent-vs-user-preference**: existing `renderChatPane(scrollToBottom=true)` callers opt INTO a scroll. Now those calls respect the user's scroll preference — the user wins. Force-bypass is only for events the user directly triggered (open pane, switch session).

**Test**: `test/bug-26-chat-auto-scroll-suppress.test.js` — 13 cases. Full sweep 63/63 green across all chat-pane tests.

runs.result:
**bug-27 fixed** as `6561681`. Two-sided fix:

**Root cause**: `state.runQueue` is a single global. `_resetUiForNewSession` cleared a dozen UI states but never touched runQueue → previous session's chips stayed in DOM until next mutation. Plus `_sendAttachSnapshot` shipped artifacts/claude-status/mode/tool-progress on attach but NOT runQueue → idle queue on the new session would never repopulate after switching back.

**Client** (`web/public/app.js _resetUiForNewSession`):
```js
state.runQueue = null;
try { _renderRunQueueStrip(); } catch {}
```
Strip hides immediately via its existing null/empty guard.

**Server** (`server/src/attach.js _sendAttachSnapshot`): ships `{ t: 'state-update', kind: 'runQueue', state: runQueue.getQueueState(rec) }` on attach, parallel to `artifacts-init`. Try/catch wrapped so getQueueState can't abort the snapshot.

**Why both sides**:
- Client-only → strip stays BLANK when switching back to an active-queue session (until next mutation). Bad UX for idle queues.
- Server-only → still flashes stale data briefly between session switch and WS frame arrival.
- Together → strip correct on the FIRST frame, every switch.

**Test**: `test/bug-27-queue-session-scope.test.js` — 7 cases (client clear + re-render, server kind:runQueue + getQueueState + try/catch + import, renderer null/empty guard). Full sweep 64/64 green.

**45 commits ahead of last deploy.**

items.text:
**Goal:** Integrate [Serena MCP](https://github.com/oraios/serena) for LSP-backed semantic code tools (`find_symbol`, `find_referencing_symbols`, `replace_symbol_body`, `rename`, `safe_delete`, ...). Complements lean-ctx (compresses reads); Serena does symbol-level navigation + editing.

**Spec (verified):** Python 3.13+ MCP server, `uv tool install -p 3.13 serena-agent@latest`. stdio (default) or HTTP. LSP backend supports 40+ languages (free, OSS); JetBrains backend skipped (paid). MIT, 24.5k stars, v1.5.1. MCP prefix: `mcp__serena__*`.

**Approach — Option A** (mirrors fr-55 lean-ctx pattern):
1. Dockerfile: install `uv` + `serena-agent` in builder. Multi-arch (uv ships amd64+arm64).
2. `agent-session.js` mcpServers: register `serena` as a 3rd stdio MCP next to `myco` + `lean-ctx`. Command: `serena`, args: `['start-mcp-server', '--project', this.cwd]`.
3. `best-practices-template.md` nudge: prefer `mcp__serena__find_symbol` / `replace_symbol_body` over grep+Edit for symbol-level ops (LSP-aware refs + renames).
4. Test: `test/fr-XX-serena-mcp.test.js`.

**5 unknowns to validate BEFORE code:**
1. Image bloat (Python 3.13 + uv + LSPs could add 500 MB+).
2. Per-session LSP indexing time (5–30s first run).
3. Memory at scale (LSP servers eat 500MB–1GB each).
4. Which LSPs pre-bake (TS/Py/JS) vs lazy.
5. Tool overlap with lean-ctx — CLAUDE.md needs precedence rule (Serena=symbols, lean-ctx=reads, native=writes).

**Out of scope v1:** JetBrains backend, force-routing Edit/Write, hybrid Serena+lean-ctx orchestration.

**Reference:** parallel to fr-55. Same Validate-First + sidecar-stdio playbook.

**Problem:** Git auth via token fails behind an enterprise proxy.
**Expected:** Token-based git auth succeeds when traffic is routed through an enterprise proxy.
**Actual:** Auth flow does not work in enterprise proxy environments.

comments.text: the bug is happening when myco is running in a container on a host behind enterprise proxy, which might cause TLS to fail.

**Investigation + design — no code yet.**

### Root cause

Enterprise proxies typically MITM HTTPS (re-sign with corporate CA). Inside the container, none of the outbound TLS callers trust that corp CA, so every TLS handshake fails with `UNABLE_TO_GET_ISSUER_CERT_LOCALLY` (or the proxy returns 502/407).

### All affected surfaces in myco today

| Surface | File:line | TLS path | Proxy env? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Anthropic API (side-channel `/btw`, `/fr!` rewrite, `/next` rerank) | `server/src/anthropic.js:27` | `https.request` default agent | none |
| GitHub/Gitee issue create (`/feature`, `/bug`) | `server/src/git-hosts.js:62` | `https.request` default agent | none |
| GitHub OAuth + user fetch | `server/src/oauth.js:57, 92` | `fetch()` default agent | none |
| `/git` slash command | `server/src/slashcmds.js handleGit` (child_process) | git's own TLS path | none |
| Agent SDK's Bash tool when it runs `git clone` | (Anthropic SDK spawns shell) | git's own | none |
| Anthropic Agent SDK itself (api.anthropic.com) | inside the SDK | Node fetch | none |
| lean-ctx `mcp` (if it ever calls out) | inside lean-ctx | Rust reqwest | none |

**Dockerfile state**: installs Alpine's stock `ca-certificates` (Mozilla CAs only). Zero proxy handling. Zero `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`. Zero custom-CA bind mount.

### What needs to land

**1. CA trust** — install corp CA into container's trust store at start, AND set `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` (Node ignores /etc/ssl/certs by default).

**2. Proxy env forwarding** — `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY`/`NO_PROXY` set in container env, inherited by every child process (Node, git, lean-ctx, future Serena, etc.).

**3. Git config (optional, belt-and-braces)** — `git config --global http.sslCAInfo /path/to/corp.pem` so git also explicitly trusts the corp CA (some setups need this in addition to system trust store).

### Two options

**Option A — Bind-mount + env (RECOMMENDED, ~3 hr)**:
- Operator drops corp CAs at `$MYCO_STATE_DIR/extra-ca-certs/*.pem` (already-bind-mounted to /data inside container)
- `.env` in state dir gets `MYCO_HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.corp:8080` etc.
- `docker-entrypoint.sh`:
  1. `cp /data/extra-ca-certs/*.pem /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ && update-ca-certificates` if any present
  2. Export `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt`
  3. Export `HTTPS_PROXY=$MYCO_HTTPS_PROXY` etc. from .env so child processes inherit
  4. `git config --global http.sslCAInfo /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt`
- `deploy.sh` post-deploy checks add: warn if state dir has extra-ca-certs/ but `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` isn't set in the running container
- Docs in CLAUDE.md Deployment section

**Option B — Full config UX (~1 day)**:
- All of A, plus slash commands: `/proxy-set <url>` / `/proxy-clear` / `/cert-add <pem>` to mutate the state-dir config from chat. Persisted in .env.
- `/qstatus`-style `/netstatus` to report current proxy/CA state.

### Validation BEFORE coding (need from you)

1. **Does the corp proxy require auth?** (`http://user:pass@proxy:8080`) — affects whether we need to mask the URL in logs
2. **What CA format does the corp ship?** (single .pem, multiple .pem, DER, PFX) — DER/PFX need conversion
3. **Does TLS-interception apply to ALL hosts or some?** `NO_PROXY` whitelist for github/anthropic might matter
4. **Container needs internet to install lean-ctx-bin + serena (postinstall downloads)** — does the proxy allow GitHub releases? If not, we need to pre-stage binaries in the image
5. **Is this for myco prod (myco.labxnow.ai), mycobeta, OR a third-party deploy of myco in someone else's corp env?** Affects whether we keep proxy support opt-in (third-party) or default-on (corp env is normal)

### Recommendation

Option A. Document the CA mount + env-var contract; ship plumbing only. Concrete proxy URL/CA bundle stays per-deployment. `deploy.sh` post-deploy validation flags misconfig.

Status: investigation only; awaiting answers to the 5 validation questions before any code lands.

items.text:
**Problem:** Clicking outside an AskUserQuestion prompt dismisses it with no way to reopen or answer until the next AskUserQuestion appears, blocking the user from responding or clarifying.
**Expected:** Outside-clicks should not dismiss the prompt, or the user should be able to re-open it / chat with the agent to provide a custom answer or deeper clarification.
**Actual:** The prompt becomes stuck and unanswerable; there is no affordance for user-specific questions or free-form clarification with the agent until another AskUserQuestion is shown.

comments.text:
**Fix shipped.** ### Two failure modes from the report **(a) Accidental dismiss via backdrop click.** Pre-fix: ```html
``` The backdrop's `data-perm-defer="1"` routed outside-clicks through the same defer handler as the X button. On touch, a thumb-edge tap that missed the dialog box dismissed it. Once dismissed, the agent's `AskUserQuestion` Promise stayed blocked but the modal was gone — user had no obvious way back, and typing in chat didn't unblock the agent (the SDK iteration was awaiting the tool-call Promise). **(b) No visible recovery affordance.** The chat-pane menu row WAS already click-to-reopen via `data-perm-reopen` + `_bindChatMenuClicks`. But the active-branch render set `optsHtml = ''` with a comment "No visible affordance line" — users didn't know the row was clickable. Until another `AskUserQuestion` fired, the session looked stuck. ### Fix - **Removed `data-perm-defer="1"`** from `.perm-modal-backdrop`. Dismiss is now explicit only — X button OR Esc key. Accidental outside-clicks are no-ops. - **Added visible `.chat-menu-reopen-hint`** pill ("↗ Tap to answer") inside active menu chat rows. The pill carries `data-perm-reopen` so the existing `_bindChatMenuClicks` delegation picks up the click and reopens the modal. ### Why not "the modal can't be dismissed at all" The user's expected said either-or. Hard-locking the modal on screen is hostile UX — if the modal pops mid-conversation and the user wants to scroll back to re-read something, they need a temporary escape. Explicit X + Esc preserves that, and the visible chat affordance preserves the recovery path. ### CSS Pill-shaped accent (blue, semi-transparent bg + 1px border) so it reads as an interactive affordance, not static text. Has `:hover` state for tactile feedback. `cursor: pointer` reinforces. ### Test `test/bug-31-modal-dismiss-recovery.test.js` — 8 cases: - HTML: `.perm-modal-backdrop` does NOT carry `data-perm-defer` - HTML: X button still does (explicit dismiss preserved) - JS: Esc still dismisses (preserved) - JS: active menu rows now render `.chat-menu-reopen-hint` with `data-perm-reopen` - JS: existing reopen wiring stays in place - CSS: pill styled with cursor:pointer + hover state - Behavior: dismiss flips flag true; reopen flips false; backdrop click is a no-op 8/8 ✓ direct. **Red-flipped against pre-fix code (3/8 failed via git stash)** — one failing test per surface (HTML backdrop, JS reopen-hint, CSS pill), confirming the test catches each piece independently. Full `./test/test.sh` aborts on host BusyBox `grep -P` (pre-existing env limit per CLAUDE.md §Pre-Commit-1). ### Notes for future work The bug-31 fix does NOT address "free-form chat with the agent for deeper clarification" — that requires the agent to include an "Other..." option in its AskUserQuestion (the existing `_permOptionIsFreeText` heuristic auto-focuses the chat input on such picks). Encouraging the agent to consistently add "Other..." is a guidance/training question, separable from bug-31's recovery UX. If the user wants to file a follow-up "always offer Other..." fr, that's a separate item. ### Takes effect on browser reload after next `./scripts/deploy.sh` push Pure web/public/ change. Status: closed.

meta.originalText: accidentially click outside the askuserquestion would stuck without opportunith to answer again until next askuserquestion show up. It doesnt have a way to provide user specific questions or allow user to chat with agent for deeper clarification.

items.text: Chat pane keep scroll to the latest msg as they come making it difficult for user who are reading the history

comments.text:
**Fix shipped (bug-26 follow-up — three uncovered paths).**

### Root cause
bug-26 added the `chatUserScrolledUp` guard to `scrollChatToLatest()` so chat-message auto-scroll respected the user's reading position. But it missed THREE direct `pane.scrollTop = pane.scrollHeight` writes inside `_appendAgentEvent` (the AGENT-event append path):

| Line | Path | Fires on |
|---|---|---|
| 4328 | chrome-batch path | Every chrome event (`canUseTool`, `hook_allow`, `hook_deny`, `system_init`, `turn_result`, etc.) |
| 4380 | assistant_text-merge path | Every streamed assistant_text token (many per second during a live turn) |
| 4486 | default append path | Every other agent event (tool calls, system messages, …) |

These three sites bypassed the bug-26 guard entirely — `pane` resolves to `#chat-messages` (the same DOM the guard protects), but the direct `scrollTop=scrollHeight` write doesn't consult `state.chatUserScrolledUp`. So even though the user's chat-bubble scroll-pin was respected, every agent-event arrival yanked them back to bottom. During a streaming assistant_text turn, reading history was effectively impossible.

### Fix
Replaced each of the three direct writes with `scrollChatToLatest()` — same DOM target, but now honors the bug-26 guard. No new helpers, no new state, no UX changes when the user IS at the bottom (the guard auto-no-ops then).

### Test
`test/bug-32-agent-event-respects-scroll.test.js` — 9 cases:
- Static guards: no `pane.scrollTop = pane.scrollHeight` writes remain in code (comment-stripped scan, allows `el.scrollTop` for the unrelated log panel)
- Static: `scrollChatToLatest` still has exactly 2 `list.scrollTop = list.scrollHeight` writes (sync + rAF pair)
- Static: `_appendAgentEvent` body calls `scrollChatToLatest` ≥3 times (one per replaced site)
- Static: `scrollChatToLatest` still respects the `!force && state.chatUserScrolledUp` guard (bug-26 contract preserved)
- Behavior simulation: 4 cases on a fake-state predicate — user-scrolled-up suppresses, user-at-bottom proceeds, force=true bypasses, 50-tick streaming-token loop all suppressed

9/9 ✓ direct. **Red-flipped against pre-fix code (3/9 failed via git stash)** — the three new strict guards trip without the fix.

bug-26's existing 13 cases still 13/13 ✓ — the chat-message path contract wasn't touched.

Full `./test/test.sh` aborts on host BusyBox `grep -P` (pre-existing env limit per CLAUDE.md §Pre-Commit-1, same as every commit since td-31).

### Takes effect on browser reload after next `./scripts/deploy.sh` push
Pure `web/public/app.js` change — no server rebuild required for correctness.

Status: closed.

items.text:
**Problem:** The file viewer path breadcrumb is cluttered by the icons sharing its row at the top.
**Expected:** Breadcrumb sits on its own dedicated row, separate from the icons.
**Actual:** Breadcrumb and icons are crammed together on the same top row.

comments.text:
**Fix shipped.**

### What changed
`#files-view-header` was a single flex row mixing 7 controls (back button + breadcrumb + spacer + 5 action buttons + 92px right-padding for the floating chrome). On narrow viewports or deep paths the row got crowded. Per the user's expected behavior, the breadcrumb now lives on its own dedicated row.

### Two-row layout

```
[← back]  path/to/some/file.js                                  ← Row 1: .files-view-header-nav
                                           [✎ Edit] [📋 Copy]    ← Row 2: .files-view-header-actions
```

- **Row 1** (`.files-view-header-nav`): back button + breadcrumb. Keeps the 92px right-padding to clear the floating `#btn-files` + `#btn-chat` chrome.
- **Row 2** (`.files-view-header-actions`): the 5 action buttons (`#files-edit`, `#files-edit-save`, `#files-edit-cancel`, `#files-wrap-toggle`, `#files-copy`). Right-aligned via `justify-content: flex-end` to match the pre-fix visual.

### Auto-hide the actions row when empty
All 5 action buttons are `hidden` by default and shown contextually (e.g. only the owner sees Edit; Save+Cancel only appear during an edit). The actions row uses:

```css
.files-view-header-actions { display: none; }
.files-view-header-actions:has(button:not([hidden])) { display: flex; }
```

So when no action button is visible (non-editable file open, fresh viewer), the row collapses to zero height — the header looks identical to a one-row "just the breadcrumb" layout for the common case. When ANY button becomes visible, the row appears under the breadcrumb. `:has()` has full modern-browser support (2023+).

### Backwards-compat
Every existing id is preserved (`#files-view-back`, `#files-view-crumbs`, `#files-edit`, …). The fr-50 tests (edit-button visibility + the file-editor flow) keep passing unchanged — 5/5 + 17/17 ✓ after the move.

- CSS: `flex-direction: column` on the header, nav row keeps 92px right padding, actions row has `display:none` + `:has()` auto-show, crumb `flex:1 / min-width:0 / overflow:hidden` for ellipsis preserved

**Third pass — mobile fix.**

### What the second pass missed
Hardcoded the top padding as `safe-area + 50px`. On desktop that's chrome-bottom(42px) + 8px buffer ✓. But `--chrome-btn-size` bumps from 32px → **40px** inside the `@media (max-width: 900px)` rule (line ~1170 of styles.css), so the chrome bottom on mobile is at `safe-area + 50px` — the breadcrumb's padding-top landed EXACTLY there. Zero buffer. Mobile still looked cluttered.

### Third-pass fix
Use the `--chrome-btn-size` variable directly so the padding auto-adapts:

```css
.files-view-header-nav {
  padding: calc(env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px)
                + 10px                       /* chrome-top */
                + var(--chrome-btn-size)     /* button height */
                + 18px                       /* buffer */
                ) 12px 8px 12px;
}
```

| Viewport | chrome-btn-size | padding-top |
|---|---|---|
| Desktop | 32px | 60px (18px buffer below chrome) |
| Mobile  | 40px | 68px (18px buffer below chrome) |
| iPhone-notch | + safe-area | same buffer (notch shifts everything together) |

### Test update
`test/bug-33-file-viewer-breadcrumb-row.test.js` guard now requires the calc() to reference BOTH `env(safe-area-inset-top)` AND `var(--chrome-btn-size)` so the layout adapts on every viewport size. Defensive negative guards rule out re-introduction of the dead first-pass (`8px 92px`) AND the dead second-pass (`+50px` hardcoded) values.

## bug-33 fourth pass — pane-level clearance

**Root cause this round:** the third-pass per-row padding was fragile under nested parent-child padding cascades on mobile (line 4061 has its own `padding: 6px 8px` on the parent `#files-view-header`; multiple `@media (max-width: 900px)` blocks both write `--chrome-btn-size`; other subtle cascades possible).

**Robust fix:** moved chrome-clearance from the inner nav row to the OUTER `#files-view-pane`:

```css
#files-view-pane {
  padding-top: calc(env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px)
                    + 10px                     /* chrome-top */
                    + var(--chrome-btn-size)   /* button height */
                    + 18px                     /* buffer */
                   );
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
.files-view-header-nav { padding: 8px 12px; }   /* normal — no clearance */
```

Whole pane shifts down. No more dependency on the inner padding cascade. Works for desktop, mobile, and iPhone-notch via the same calc. Future rows added inside the pane automatically inherit the clearance.

**Cache-bust note:** after redeploy, hard-refresh (Cmd+Shift+R desktop / "Clear and reload" mobile Safari/Chrome) — browser CSS cache will otherwise hold the stale stylesheet, which may explain why the prior pass appeared to not take effect.

## UX improvements for the plan view (your follow-up ask)

The plan view is your central workspace now. Here's a prioritized list of high-impact UX improvements, **mobile-first**:

### Tier 1 — Mobile readability + thumb ergonomics (likely highest impact)

1. **Sticky filter row on scroll.** Today fr-56's filter chips + search input scroll away with the list. On a 600-item plan they're gone after one swipe. Make `#plan-filter-row` `position: sticky; top: 0` so type/search controls stay reach

items.text:
**Problem:** Plan item view's create time is ambiguous because it only shows time-of-day.
**Expected:** Create time displays the date (and time).
**Actual:** Only the time of day is shown, with no date.

comments.text:
**Fix shipped.**

### What changed
Plan-item byline was `formatChatTs(it.addedAt)` — `toLocaleTimeString` returns only HH:MM. Fine for chat bubbles in a same-day session but ambiguous for plan items days/weeks/months old: two items filed at "14:32" on different days looked identical.

New helper `formatChatTsWithDate(iso)` uses `toLocaleString` with `year + month + day + hour + minute` options. Example output:

```
filed by @kkrazy · May 23, 2026, 14:32
```

Wired into the plan-item byline at `renderArtifact > renderItem` (around line 6568 of `web/public/app.js`). The hover title still shows the raw ISO for precise inspection.

### Scope
**Only the plan-item byline.** Three other call sites still use the time-only `formatChatTs`:
- Chat-bubble timestamps (same-day usage, time-only is the right shape)
- Plan-item COMMENT timestamps (different visual context, not in bug-34 report)
- Artifact "Updated" banner timestamps (different visual context, not in bug-34 report)

If the same ambiguity bites for comments or "Updated" banners, file a follow-up and switch those sites too. Per CLAUDE.md §8.4 surgical-edits discipline.

### Null-safety
`new Date(null)` resolves to epoch (Jan 1, 1970) — a clear failure mode for a "filed at" line. The new helper short-circuits on `null` / `undefined` / `''` and returns `''` so the byline cleanly omits the timestamp instead of showing 1970.

### Test
`test/bug-34-plan-item-create-time-shows-date.test.js` — 9 cases:
- Static: helper exists, uses `toLocaleString` (not `toLocaleTimeString`), requires year+month+day+hour+minute options, is null-safe
- Static: plan-item byline calls the new helper; negative guard that the OLD `formatChatTs(it.addedAt)` call is gone
- Behavior simulation on fixed ISO inputs: valid → contains year + HH:MM + month, null/undefined/"" → "", invalid → "", **bug-34 repro**: two items same time-of-day different days produce DIFFERENT strings
- Scope tripwire: `formatChatTs` still has ≥3 call sites (chat bubbles + comments + Updated banner) so the fix didn't accidentally sweep beyond the bug-34 scope

9/9 ✓ direct. **Red-flipped against pre-fix code (4/9 failed via git stash)** — confirms the test catches the regression.

### Three more sites switched to formatChatTsWithDate
| Site | Was | Now |
|---|---|---|
| Plan-item comment ts (line ~6562) | `formatChatTs(c.ts)` | `formatChatTsWithDate(c.ts)` |
| Arch tab "Updated XXX" banner (line ~6464) | `formatChatTs(artifact.updatedAt)` | `formatChatTsWithDate(artifact.updatedAt)` |

items.text:
**Problem:** Queued plan items still pause mid-run for user input, breaking the autopilot expectation.
**Expected:** When submitting a plan item to the queue, gather all clarifying questions up front, then execute to completion without further interaction.
**Actual:** Execution stops partway to ask questions that could have been asked at submission time.

comments.text:
**Investigation (no code yet) — this is a behavioral change with real trade-offs.**

### Where mid-run pauses come from
Three sources today, all reachable from a queued run:

| Source | Path | What pauses the agent |
|---|---|---|
| **`AskUserQuestion` tool** | `agent-session.js _handleAskUserQuestion` → modal popup | Agent decided it needs clarification. SDK Promise blocks until user clicks an answer. |
| **Permission requests** (canUseTool) | `agent-session.js _handlePermissionRequest` → 3-option chat menu | Tool not in allow/deny list. Agent waits for [Allow once / Allow always / Deny]. |
| **Inline questions in chat text** | Agent writes "should I do X or Y?" as a regular assistant message | Soft pause — no SDK lock, but the agent typically waits for the next user turn. |

The queue dispatch (`buildArtifactRunText`) currently has no signal that distinguishes a queued run from an interactive one — the agent treats both the same and follows the same CLAUDE.md guidance (which actually *encourages* clarifying questions per §8.1).

### What "queued" means today
Queue dispatch → `handleChatMessage` → text with `[run:plan#<id>]` marker → agent gets it in chat → agent acts as normal. The marker only binds `turn_result` back to the queue entry; it doesn't change behavior.

### Four options for "autopilot mode"

**Option A — In-prompt directive (simplest, ~1 hr)**
`buildArtifactRunText` prepends an "autopilot mode" instruction:

> *This was dispatched via the run-queue. You're in autopilot mode: make best-judgment assumptions, document them in your reply, and complete the work without asking clarifying questions or waiting for further input. If a decision is truly blocking and you can't make a reasonable assumption, fail loudly with a clear "blocked by: <reason>" message and stop.*

Trade-offs:
- ✓ Cheap, no SDK/server changes beyond text-building
- ✓ Agent retains judgment — can still STOP for genuinely blocking issues
- ✗ Soft contract — relies on the agent obeying. Some agent flavors / SDK versions may still pop `AskUserQuestion` despite the instruction.
- ✗ Doesn't handle permission requests — if a queued run hits an unapproved tool, the chat menu still pops.

**Option B — Two-phase: gather-questions first, then queue (matches user's "Expected" verbatim)**
- User clicks ▶ Run → server sends a special "scoping" dispatch: *"Read this item. List ANY clarifying questions you'd need to finish it. Stop after listing — do not write code yet."*
- Agent replies with question list (or "no questions; ready to run")
- A new "▶ Queue with answers" button appears
- User answers in a single form, hits "Queue"
- THEN the queue dispatches the item with the Q&A pasted in as context

Trade-offs:
- ✓ Matches the user's Expected behavior exactly
- ✓ Strong contract — the autopilot is the SECOND turn, after questions are answered
- ✗ Doubles the chat round-trips per queued item
- ✗ Needs new UI state ("scoping" / "awaiting answers" between Pending and Running)
- ✗ Significant scope — ~half day

**Option C — Tool-level enforcement (hard contract, ~2 hr)**
- Set `session._queuedMode = true` when dispatching from the queue
- In `_canUseTool`, when `_queuedMode` is on:
  - `AskUserQuestion` → auto-resolve with empty answers (agent gets a clear "no answers" signal and must proceed)
  - Permission requests → auto-deny with the reason "blocked: queued runs don't prompt for permissions; add an allow rule first"
- `_queuedMode` clears when the queue entry's terminal event lands

Trade-offs:
- ✓ Hard guarantee — agent literally cannot pause
- ✓ Surfaces missing permission rules upfront (blocked items pause the queue cleanly)
- ✗ Aggressive — a genuinely-needed clarification dies silently; the agent might just plow forward with a wrong assumption
- ✗ Permission auto-deny will fail queued items that need tools the user hasn't pre-approved

**Option D — Hybrid: A + C, with Option C as the safety net**
- In-prompt directive (Option A) tells the agent to behave
- Tool-level enforcement (Option C) catches it if it doesn't
- Best of both: agent has agency to STOP cleanly with a clear failure message, but if it tries to ask anyway, the system makes that fail fast

### My recommendation: **Option D**
Reasons:
1. Option A alone is too soft — different SDK / model versions may not obey
2. Option B is the most "correct" UX but doubles every queued run's turns; high cost for a maybe-rare scenario (most plan items DON'T need clarification)
3. Option C alone risks silent bad-assumption work
4. Option D = soft guidance (agent decides whether to STOP cleanly with a "blocked by X" failure) + hard backstop (tool can't actually pop a modal). The queue stays moving; failures are visible.

### Test plan (for any option)
- Static guards: `buildArtifactRunText` includes the autopilot directive when called with item.id; `_canUseTool` checks `_queuedMode`
- Behavior simulation: fake session with `_queuedMode=true` → `AskUserQuestion` resolves with empty answers; permission request resolves with deny
- Integration: dispatch a queued item; assert agent doesn't pop a modal even when it would have

### Estimated effort
- A alone: ~1 hr
- C alone: ~2 hr
- D: ~3 hr
- B: ~half day + UI work

### Questions for you
1. **Which option** — A, B, C, D, or a different shape entirely?
2. **When the agent encounters genuine ambiguity** in autopilot mode, what should happen?
   - **Best-guess + document**: agent picks the most reasonable option, notes the assumption in its reply, proceeds
   - **Hard-stop**: agent marks the queue entry failed with "blocked: <reason>", queue auto-pauses for triage
   - **Best-guess + flag**: agent proceeds but appends a "⚠ ambiguous decisions made — review" footer to its reply
3. **Permission requests**: same question — auto-deny (fail loud) or auto-allow (yolo)? Current proposal is auto-deny.

Status: investigation only, awaiting Option pick.

meta.originalText: for plan items added to the queue, it should try to run to its completion without user interaction. Should ask up front question when submitting to the queue and then go into autopilot mode

items.text: Plan tab: sticky filter row on scroll. Today fr-56's type chips + search input scroll away after one swipe on a long plan list (119+ items now). Make #plan-filter-row `position: sticky; top: 0` with the artifact-body background so the filters stay reachable while scrolling. CSS-only change. Test: static guard that `position: sticky` is set on #plan-filter-row.

comments.text:
**Shipped.**

### What the user gets
fr-56's type chips (🐞 Bug / ✨ Feature / ✅ Todo) + search input now stay pinned to the top of the plan list while you scroll through items. No more swiping back up to retype a search or toggle a type filter.

### Why "CSS-only" wasn't actually possible
The fr-61 description called for CSS-only, but `#plan-filter-row` is a **sibling** of the scrollable body (`#artifact-body-plan`), not a descendant. `position: sticky` only pins against the closest scrolling ancestor — sibling-to-scrolling-element doesn't work; the row would silently behave like `static`.

Two-part fix:
1. **CSS** (`styles.css`): `#plan-filter-row { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 5; background: var(--bg); margin: -12px -14px 0 -14px; }`. Negative margins counter the body's padding so the sticky row spans the full body width (clean strip look).
2. **JS** (`app.js`): new `_attachPlanFilterRowToBody(body, type)` helper relocates `#plan-filter-row` from its static-HTML position (sibling of body) into the body's first-child slot. Called after every plan-path `body.innerHTML` write so the row survives the wipe.

### Idempotent + scoped
- Helper short-circuits when `type !== 'plan'` (other artifact tabs unaffected)
- Helper checks `firstElementChild` before re-inserting (no DOM churn on no-op calls)
- Existing event listeners on the chips + search input survive the move (`insertBefore` preserves them)

### Test
`test/fr-61-plan-filter-row-sticky.test.js` — 9 cases:
- CSS: `#plan-filter-row` declares `position: sticky` + `top: 0` + opaque background + z-index
- JS helper exists; no-op on non-plan; uses `insertBefore(filterRow, body.firstChild)`; idempotent via `firstElementChild` check
- Call sites: ≥4 references to the helper (definition + ≥3 callers — one per plan-path `body.innerHTML` write site)
- HTML: `#plan-filter-row` still in static markup (the helper assumes it exists)

9/9 ✓ direct. **Red-flipped against pre-fix CSS** (3/9 fail when sticky declaration absent). All 5 adjacent plan-tab tests stay green: fr-56-plan-filter-search 20/20 ✓, fr-64-plan-item-status-chip 16/16 ✓, fr-65-plan-layer-done-accordion 12/12 ✓, fr-62-plan-item-mobile-tap-targets 9/9 ✓, plan-open-only-toggle 9/9 ✓.

Status: closed.

**Reopened + fixed (second pass).**

### User report
"The plan view filter for bug/fr/tod and search disappears after switch tabs."

### Root cause
First-pass fix moved `#plan-filter-row` INTO `#artifact-body-plan` so `position: sticky` works. `_attachPlanFilterRowToBody` ran AFTER each `body.innerHTML` write to re-insert the row.

But the helper looks up `#plan-filter-row` by id at run-time:
```js
const filterRow = document.getElementById('plan-filter-row');
if (!filterRow) return;   // early-return — bug-31 starts here
```

After `body.innerHTML = ...` wiped the body, the filter row was destroyed (it was inside the body). The post-wipe helper had nothing to find and returned silently. Filter row gone, no recovery on subsequent renders.

This hit the user on **tab switches** (switching away from plan, switching back triggers a fresh `renderArtifact('plan')` which wipes-then-tries-to-reattach), **session switches** (`clearArtifactBodies` wipes every artifact body), and **session deletes** (same path).

### Second-pass fix — stash-before-wipe pattern
New helper `_stashPlanFilterRow()` that rescues the row back to its original static-HTML parent (`#plan-wrap`) BEFORE the wipe. Then `_attachPlanFilterRowToBody` after the wipe finds the rescued row at `#plan-wrap` and re-inserts it at the body's first child.

```js
_stashPlanFilterRow();           // rescue: body → plan-wrap
body.innerHTML = newContent;     // wipe is safe now
_attachPlanFilterRowToBody(...); // restore: plan-wrap → body
```

Called before each of the 4 plan-path `body.innerHTML` writes AND in `clearArtifactBodies` (session-switch / delete paths).

### Test
`test/fr-61-plan-filter-row-sticky.test.js` extended from 9 → 13 cases:
- `_stashPlanFilterRow` helper exists
- Restores to `#plan-wrap` (not anywhere else)
- ≥5 references total (definition + ≥4 call sites — one before each wipe)
- `clearArtifactBodies` includes the rescue call

13/13 ✓ direct. **Red-flipped against pre-fix code (4/13 failed via git stash)** — one failure per surface, confirming each call site is independently enforced.

items.text: Plan items: bigger tap targets on mobile. Vote / comment / Run / Close buttons are ~24px today — Apple HIG + Material both call for 44px minimum. Bump button hit area (padding + min-size) on mobile only. Eliminates pinch-to-tap on phones. CSS-only inside `@media (max-width: 900px)`. Test: grep guards that artifact-vote / artifact-comment-toggle / artifact-run / artifact-close min-size ≥ 44px in the mobile block.

comments.text:
**Shipped.**

### What changed
Inside `@media (max-width: 900px)`, every plan-item action button gets a 44×44px minimum hit area:

```css
@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .artifact-item-actions {
    gap: 10px;        /* was 6px — adjacent buttons stay distinct at the bigger tap size */
  }
  .artifact-vote,
  .artifact-comment-toggle,
  .artifact-item-run,
  .artifact-item-close,
  .artifact-item-delete,
  .artifact-item-edit,
  .artifact-comment-edit,
  .artifact-comment-delete {
    min-height: 44px;
    min-width: 44px;
    padding-top: 8px;
    padding-bottom: 8px;
  }
}
```

### Why 44px
Apple HIG and Material Design both call for ≥44pt/dp minimum hit area on touch surfaces. Pre-fix, our action buttons were ~22–24px tall on mobile — well below the threshold, leading to pinch-to-tap and accidental-neighbor-hit on phones.

### Desktop unchanged
The bump is mobile-only. Desktop precision-pointing keeps the compact ~24px size — no visual bloat for the keyboard-and-mouse case. A negative guard in the test pins that the base (non-media) `.artifact-vote` rule does NOT carry `min-height: 44px`.

### 8 buttons covered
- `.artifact-vote` (👍 vote)
- `.artifact-comment-toggle` (💬 comments)
- `.artifact-item-run` (▶ Run/Fix/Implement/Do)
- `.artifact-item-close` (Close/Reopen)
- `.artifact-item-delete` (× delete)
- `.artifact-item-edit` (✎ edit body)
- `.artifact-comment-edit` (✎ edit comment)
- `.artifact-comment-delete` (× delete comment)

### Test

comments.text:
**Shipped.**

### What you see now
Each plan-item row leads with a small color-coded status chip:

```
[▶  bug-42]  Live-reload subagent dispatch                ← running
[⏸  bug-43]  WS reconnect storm fix                       ← queued
[🟢 bug-12]  Re-entry restore chat pane                   ← closed
[📌 fr-58]   Lean-ctx Serena MCP integration              ← in-progress (has runs)
[⚪ bug-90]  Random Tuesday Tab keyboard bug              ← open
```

### State derivation (no schema change)
Single function `_planItemStatus(it, runQueueEntries)` with strict precedence (first match wins):

1. **running** — `state.runQueue.entries` has this id with `status==='running'`
2. **queued** — same, with `status==='pending'`
3. **closed** — `it.done` truthy
4. **inprogress** — `it.runs[]` non-empty AND not active/done (signal: "I've worked on this before")
5. **open** — fallthrough default

The 📌 (pinned glyph) is repurposed for "in-progress / touched at least once" — pure data-derived, no new schema. If you'd rather use 📌 for an explicit user-pin field, that's a separate fr that adds `it.pinned` to the schema.

### Plan-only
Gated on `supportsVoting` so the chip only renders for the Plan tab, not Test (which has no queue/done lifecycle to surface).

### Reactivity
Chip updates on every `renderArtifact('plan', …)` call — same triggers as bug-15 / fr-56 / fr-65: queue state changes (`state-update` WS frame), item.done flips, run completion, etc. No new event wiring needed.

### Files touched
- `web/public/app.js`: 3 helpers (`_planItemStatus`, `_planItemStatusChipHtml`, `_PLAN_ITEM_STATUS_MAP`) in the fr-56/fr-65 helper region; `statusChip` computed inside `renderItem` and injected at the start of `.artifact-item-row` (before `idChip`).
- `web/public/styles.css`: `.artifact-item-status-chip` base rule + five `.is-*` modifier rules with distinct `border-left-color` per status.

### Test
`test/fr-64-plan-item-status-chip.test.js` — 16 cases:
- Static: 3 helpers + the `_PLAN_ITEM_STATUS_MAP` constant; each of 5 states present in the map with glyph + label + cls; chip placement at start of row; `supportsVoting` gate; defensive null-fallback for missing runQueue
- CSS: base rule + 5 per-status modifier rules
- Behavior simulation: 9 cases on a reference implementation pinning the precedence (running > queued > closed > inprogress > open), each state derivation, and null-fallback safety

items.text: Plan items: inline run-history sparkline. Items with multiple runs (recurring bugs like bug-33) show a compact `●●●●` strip color-coded by each run's status (green=success, red=failed, yellow=aborted). Hover/tap tooltip names the run + ts. Surfaces iteration pattern without expanding the comment thread. Reads from item.runs[]. Test: rendering asserts N dots == N runs + color class matches each status.

Plan tab: '/' focuses search, Esc clears. Single-character global hotkey listener (GitHub-style). When user presses '/' (and no input is focused), focus the fr-56 #plan-search input. When Esc is pressed inside #plan-search, clear the query + blur. Fast triage shortcut. Test: keydown handler bound + handler short-circuits when target is an input/textarea.

Plan tab: ↑/↓ navigate items, Enter opens. Vim-style list navigation — after pressing '/' (fr-XX above) cursor up/down through the FILTERED list, Enter expands the item's comment thread. Visual focus ring on the active row. Depends on the '/' shortcut + a state.planFocusedItemId field. Test: static guards on the keydown branches + state-effect simulation.

Plan tab: '× Clear filters' button in empty state. When fr-56 filters narrow to zero matches, the empty-state message names which filters are active but offers no escape — add a one-click reset that clears type chips (re-enables all 3) + clears search input. Tiny UX win for the dead-end case. CSS + handler. ~15 min. Test: static guard the button appears in the empty-state branch.

Plan layers: visual separation via indent + colored accent strip. Today Bug/Feature/Todo render as flat text headers between items; hurts scannability on mobile. Add a 3px-wide colored vertical accent strip on each layer's items (red=Bug, blue=Feature, green=Todo) + 4px left indent. CSS-only. ~20 min. Test: per-layer color rule + indent assertion.

'Copy link' affordance next to the existing comment-toggle. Read URL hash on plan-tab open + on artifact re-render. Useful for sharing in chat with collaborators. ~45 min. Test: hash-parse helper + render-highlight asserts.

Plan items: swipe gestures on mobile (left=Run, right=Close/Reopen). Touch gesture: swipe an item-row left → reveal a Run button + tap or full-swipe to dispatch. Swipe right → reveal a Close/Reopen button. Matches iOS Mail / Things / Linear patterns. Shared touchstart/touchmove/touchend handler. Owner+admin only (read-only viewers should not swipe-mutate). ~2 hours. Test: handler binding + state-mutation guards.

Plan items: long-press multi-select + 'Queue N selected' bulk action. Long-press a plan item enters selection mode (checkbox per item, header turns into an action bar). Tap others to add to selection. 'Queue all (N)' button dispatches every selected item to the run-queue in order. Common workflow ('dispatch the next 5 bugs') today requires 5 separate Run clicks. ~2 hours. Owner+admin only. Test: state machine for selection mode + bulk-dispatch loop.

Plan items: drag-to-reorder priority within a layer. Add a numeric `priority` field on each plan item (default 0); items within each layer sort by priority desc, then by addedAt. Touch + mouse drag handler swaps two items' priority values. Server persists via /artifact/item/edit (already exists). Combined with fr-56 type-filter this lets users build a working triage queue by drag. ~2 hours. Test: priority schema + sort assertion + drag-handler unit test.

runs.summary: r1 (@kkrazy comment: 'the issue creation works now, but it's not rewriting it to proper format before submitting the issue'). Root cause: the remote-target branch in handleAddPlanItem (slashcmds.js ~line 363) was computing shouldRewrite via the same threshold expression as local plan items: `forceRewrite || wordCount > PLAN_ITEM_REWRITE_WORD_THRESHOLD`. The threshold (8 words) made sense for local quick captures — `/td auth flow broken` stays quick because it's just a local memo — but for remote issues the trade-off flips: a short remote capture still goes to a public bug tracker where Problem/Expected/Actual shape is always worth the extra rewrite call. fr-80 r5 had already added the rewrite path but gated it behind the same threshold; @kkrazy's r1 report surfaces that short captures (≤8 words, no `!`) were still landing raw on GitHub. Fix: in the remote branch ONLY, `shouldRewrite = true` unconditionally. The local-plan-item branch a few lines below keeps the threshold so local quick captures stay quick (verified by the fr-81 r1 test's second case + the existing fr-80 r5 test updated to reflect the new contract). Regression test: test/fr-81-r1-always-rewrite-remote.test.js (3 cases) — locks the remote-branch shouldRewrite is `= true` (not the threshold expression), the LOCAL-branch shouldRewrite still uses the threshold (didn't regress quick captures), and a fr-81 marker comment exists. Wired into test/test.sh after fr-94. The existing fr-80 r5 test (test/fr-80-remote-issue-routing.test.js) was updated to match the new contract: its 'must compute shouldRewrite (forceRewrite || wordCount > threshold)' assertion became 'must compute shouldRewrite = true' for the remote branch (now passes 19/19 instead of 18/19). Not yet committed/pushed/deployed — awaiting user direction.

**Actual:** No drawing affordance exists on the chat field; diagrams must be created externally and attached as images.

runs.result:
**fr-84 shipped end-to-end.**

To verify on opti.labxnow.ai (hard-refresh to bust cache):
1. Click the new **Draw** button between Speak and Send in the chat composer
2. Modal opens with 5 tools (pen / rect / ellipse / line / text), 4 colors, 3 stroke widths
3. Draw something → click **Insert** (or Ctrl/Cmd+Enter)
4. Modal closes, `![diagram](/sessions/<id>/diagrams/...svg)` appears in the chat input
5. Hit Send — the diagram renders inline in chat for every attached viewer

**Highlights:**
- **Zero new vendor weight** — no Excalidraw, no tldraw, no fabric, no paper, no konva. Pure browser-native SVG + mouse/touch events. The whole engine is ~250 lines of vanilla JS.
- **All shapes are real SVG elements** on a `<g>` layer, so undo just pops the last child and save just serializes the live DOM with `XMLSerializer` — no rasterization, no canvas-to-PNG.
- **Storage**: `_myco_/diagrams/<ts>-<hex>.svg` — travels with the repo on commit (matches the shared-project-memory rule).
- **Server**: POST owner-only with strict SVG validation (start `<svg>`, end `</svg>`, no `<script>`, ≤ 512 KB). GET viewer-readable with strict filename whitelist (blocks path traversal) and `Content-Type: image/svg+xml`.
- **Keyboard**: Esc closes, Ctrl/Cmd+Z undoes, Ctrl/Cmd+Enter inserts.
- **Mobile**: `touch-action: none` on the canvas so dragging doesn't accidentally pan the page.
- **Tests**: `fr-84-diagram-tool.test.js` — 12 cases including a "no heavy diagramming lib imports" guard so the "as light as possible" constraint can't silently regress.
- **v0.0.4 rolled forward** to `9b37e97` (annotated SHA `befdb80`).

Two different stories for the two providers. Short answer:

| Provider | Need to register an OAuth app? | What you'd register / where |
|---|---|---|
| **GitHub** | **Yes**, IF you want new users to be able to **log in to myco** | GitHub Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps → New OAuth App |
| **Gitee** | **No** | Gitee has no OAuth flow in myco — Gitee PATs are set manually per-repo via the new Config page (or `/setpat`) |

## Why GitHub needs an OAuth App

Looking at `server/src/index.js` + `auth.js`:

- The **myco login** path is `GET /auth/github/start` → redirects to `https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize?client_id=<MYCO_GH_CLIENT_ID>&…`
- That route returns **HTTP 500** when `MYCO_GH_CLIENT_ID` isn't set — that's the warning you've seen on every deploy (`OAuth not configu…

runs.result:
## fr-87 (config page) landed — `a41e18c`

```
feat(fr-87): Config page MVP — let regular users manage their own PATs
 7 files changed, 845 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)
```

### What the user sees

**Click the `@username` chip in the top-right status bar** → Config modal opens.

The modal shows:
- **Personal access tokens** section
  - Per-provider user-level rows (github + gitee), each showing `••••XXXX` or `(none)` with Set / Delete buttons
  - Per-repo rows (one per stored entry), each labeled `github/owner/repo` or `github/owner/repo#alias`, with Set / Delete buttons
  - "Add a per-repo PAT" form at the bottom (provider, owner, repo, alias, token input)
- **Account** section
  - Sign Out button (relocated from the legacy chip-click `confirm()` prompt)

Token values are **never** shipped to the client — only `last4` + `present:bool`.

### Security contract locked in tests

The 17-case regression test explicitly asserts:
- `maskToken` returns only `{present, last4}` — no `raw`/`token`/`value` field
- `listAllPats` output JSON-serialized **never** contains any substring of any raw token (`SECRET` marker test)
- `GET /config/pats` handler **never** calls `getToken` (which returns raw values)
- All 5 routes require auth
- A comment in `index.js` explicitly names fr-87 + the never-leak invariant so future "simplifications" don't reintroduce a leak

### Local state

- HEAD = `a41e18c` (1 commit ahead of origin)
- Unstaged: only the `_myco_/plan.json` drift from the `[run:plan#fr-87]` dispatch

### Open follow-ups (your call)

1. Push `a41e18c` to origin? (Same `GIT_ASKPASS` pattern; PAT still in chat — please rotate when done.)
2. Deploy to `myco.labxnow.ai`? (Tarball + `--skip-tests` local-mode-on-host like the last two deploys.)
3. `memory: sync` for the plan.json drift?

Per CLAUDE.md §0 I'm holding for explicit "deploy" before touching prod.

Two different stories for the two providers. Short answer:

| Provider | Need to register an OAuth app? | What you'd register / where |
|---|---|---|
| **GitHub** | **Yes**, IF you want new users to be able to **log in to myco** | GitHub Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps → New OAuth App |
| **Gitee** | **No** | Gitee has no OAuth flow in myco — Gitee PATs are set manually per-repo via the new Config page (or `/setpat`) |

## Why GitHub needs an OAuth App

Looking at `server/src/index.js` + `auth.js`:

- The **myco login** path is `GET /auth/github/start` → redirects to `https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize?client_id=<MYCO_GH_CLIENT_ID>&…`
- That route returns **HTTP 500** when `MYCO_GH_CLIENT_ID` isn't set — that's the warning you've seen on every deploy (`OAuth not configured (missing in .env: MYCO_GH_CLIENT_ID …)`)
- Without that env var, new users can't authenticate to myco itself. The only people who can log in are users whose tokens are already in `auth-sessions.json` from a *prior* OAuth setup that ran somewhere
- After a successful GitHub OAuth login, the callback **auto-mirrors the user's GitHub access_token into `/data/git-tokens.json`** at the user-level `github` slot — so the user gets a fallback PAT for free. Per-repo PATs from the Config page just override that for specific repos.

So the GitHub OAuth App is doing two jobs at once:
1. **Authentication** — gates who can use myco at all (combined with `allowed-github-users.txt`)
2. **User-level GitHub PAT auto-issuance** — populates the GitHub fallback token so `/feature`, `/bug`, claude's git tool, etc. can clone/push private repos without forcing every user to `/setpat`

## Exact steps to register a GitHub OAuth App

If you want to wire login for `myco.labxnow.ai`:

1. Go to **https://github.com/settings/developers** → **OAuth Apps** → **New OAuth App** (this is a personal/user OAuth App — for an org-owned app, use the org's `Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps` instead)
2. Fill in:

items.description:
**Problem:** There is no persistent store for the critic rules that actually prove effective during real runs, so learnings are lost between sessions.
**Expected:** A `critic.md` file that auto-accumulates the most effective critic rules (e.g. anti-patterns) and grows organically based on real system results.
**Actual:** No `critic.md` exists; effective rules and anti-patterns are not captured or reused.
**Context:** Markdown file intended to grow over time, driven by observed outcomes of the system.

runs.result:
**fr-89 Phase 1 implemented + tested.** Awaiting full test.sh background completion.

**What shipped:**

- **NEW** `server/templates/critic.md` — researched myco-shipped default with 4 sections:
  - Core review principles (anchor on task, severity, citations, no invented context, match user's ask)
  - Anti-patterns to flag (swallowed errors, speculative APIs, hard deletes, weakened tests, regex-on-SDK-events, magic numbers, duplicated state, mismatched comments)
  - Things NOT to flag (calibration — trivial CSS, trail markers, plan.json edits, adjacent unrelated code, legit dead-code removal)

  - `_loadProjectCriticRules(absCwd)` reads `<project>/_myco_/critic.md`

  - Loaded content appended to systemPrompt under a clearly-labelled "extends but never overrides" header

items.text: Track critic accuracy by domain and route plans to best-performing model

comments.text: At the begining rotate the critic across all available models in a round robin manner until a good profiling is available for targeted dispatch
Research and create an intial critic.md

meta.originalText: keep a list of critics and record the critic results that have been right or wrong. Use the record to identify what model is good at what domain  and dispath a plan to the most capable model in that domain

items.description:
**Problem:** Plans are dispatched without regard to which critic/model is historically reliable in the relevant domain, leading to suboptimal routing.
**Expected:** Maintain a registry of critics with per-domain right/wrong records, and dispatch each plan to the model with the strongest track record in that domain.
**Actual:** No critic results are persisted and no domain-aware model selection exists; dispatch is uninformed by past correctness.
**Context:** Requires (1) a critic list, (2) a results log capturing right/wrong outcomes tagged by domain, and (3) a dispatcher that queries the log to pick the most capable model per domain.

items.description:
**Problem:** Users cannot upvote plan items — the vote request is rejected by the server.
**Expected:** `POST /sessions/:sessionId/artifact/vote?type=plan&itemId=...` succeeds and records the upvote.
**Actual:** Request returns `403 (Forbidden)`, so the upvote never registers.
**Context:** Repro at `app.js?v=2026-06-01T23:11:40Z:95`, session `myco-kkrazy-f80476dd`, item `bug-7`, host `mycobeta.labxnow.ai`.

runs.result:
**bug-46 ready to commit.** Working tree:

```
?? test/bug-46-vote-cross-user.test.js
 M server/src/index.js          ← +1 'authed' tier branch in fileApiPreamble
 M server/src/artifacts.js      ← vote endpoint: 'viewer' → 'authed' + bug-46 comment
 M test/test.sh                 ← wire-in
 M _myco_/plan.json             ← bug-46 run stamped completed
```

**Test results:** bug-46 4/4 green; adjacent suites untouched (fr-26 10/10, bug-45 26/26).

**The fix in one sentence:** `/artifact/vote` now uses the new `'authed'` tier in `fileApiPreamble` — auth still required (401 for anonymous), but session-access check is bypassed. Every other endpoint (chat, queue mutations, file API, run dispatch) stays on owner/viewer per fr-87.

**Separate concern — your Resume button report:**

Looking at the code, `/queue/resume` (`artifacts.js:933`) has **two** gates:
1. `fileApiPreamble('viewer')` — fr-87 private-by-default check
2. Explicit `isOwnerOrAdmin(ctx.id, user)` — even viewers can't resume

So unlike vote, the design is "only the session owner can resume the queue." The 403 is intentional at the server. The bug is that the client renders the Resume button without checking owner status. Two ways to fix:

**Option A — hide Resume for non-owners (UI fix, preserves owner-only semantics).** The `_renderRunQueueStrip` (`app.js:7138`) currently gates on `!state.readOnly`. We add a check for `state.isOwner` (or compute it from the session record). Server stays strict.

**Option B — open Resume to any authed user (server change, collaborative queue).** Drops the `isOwnerOrAdmin` check on `/queue/resume`. Anyone signed-in can resume. Similar move as bug-46 vote.

Which way for Resume? (Also do you want this rolled into the same commit as bug-46, or a separate round?)

Awaiting direction on Resume + the standard `commit + push + deploy` for bug-46.

**Commit `dbc06ed` pushed** to `kkrazy/myco main`. Two distinct concerns shipped together:

**1. bug-46 — cross-user vote + comment (your dispatch + follow-up)**
- New `'authed'` tier in `fileApiPreamble` (server/src/index.js): auth required, but no owner/admin/viewer/share-token check
- `/artifact/vote` → `'authed'` (per the run dispatch)
- `/artifact/comment` → `'authed'` (per "anyone logged in should be able to comment")
- Every other endpoint (chat, queue mutations, file API) stays on `'owner'`/`'viewer'` per fr-87
- Marker comments call out that `'authed'` is the single carve-out — not a general loosening
- 5/5 test cases

**2. Critic calibration (precondition for re-reviewing)**
- Model: `gemini-1.5-pro` (retired by Google on v1beta) → `gemini-2.5-flash`
- Sampling: explicit `temperature: 0.2`, `topP: 0.8`, `maxOutputTokens: 1024` (was Gemini defaults tuned for chat)
- System prompt: added `INSUFFICIENT INFORMATION:` opt-out + explicit "you only see the diff" clause
- All values pulled into `CRITIC_MODEL` + `CRITIC_SAMPLING` named constants so the next bump is one line
- 9/9 test cases

**Not addressed:**
- **Resume button 403** — still owner-only by design on the server (`isOwnerOrAdmin` check). Needs a separate round: hide the button for non-owners (UI fix) OR open `/queue/resume` to all authed users (server change). Waiting on your direction from the earlier ask.

Standing by for `commit + push + deploy` — actually pushed already, awaiting **deploy** direction (and the Resume button decision when you're ready).

Understood — holding the deploy. Here's the diff at a glance so you know what's in `dbc06ed`:

```
 _myco_/plan.json                       | 110 ++++++++++++++++++- (bug-46 run stamp)
 server/src/index.js                    |  29 ++++++- (+'authed' tier in fileApiPreamble)
 server/src/artifacts.js                |  24 +++++- (vote + comment → 'authed' tier)
 server/src/critics/gemini.js           |  37 +++++++-- (model + sampling)
 server/src/critique.js                 |  18 ++++- (INSUFFICIENT INFORMATION opt-out)
 test/bug-46-vote-cross-user.test.js    | 132 +++ (NEW — 5 cases)
 test/critic-gemini-calibration.test.js | 137 +++ (NEW — 9 cases)
 test/test.sh                           |  28 +++++ (wire-ins)
```

**Quick links for review:**
- The diff on GitHub: `https://github.com/kkrazy/myco/commit/dbc06ed`
- The "authed" tier branch: `server/src/index.js` ~line 793-815 (new code) inside `fileApiPreamble`
- Vote + comment switch: `server/src/artifacts.js` ~line 619 (vote) and ~652 (comment)
- Critic model bump + sampling: `server/src/critics/gemini.js` (full rewrite of the small file)
- Critic prompt opt-out: `server/src/critique.js` ~line 20 (the system prompt rewrite)

**Things worth a second look that I'd flag myself:**

1. **`'authed'` tier is intentionally a session-id-knower bypass.** Anyone signed in who knows the 8-hex session id can vote/comment. If your threat model treats session ids as sensitive (they leak via `state.activeId` in client-side URLs / WS frames), this carve-out widens that surface. Counterpoint: voting is idempotent toggle, comments are append-only, no other state can be mutated through these endpoints.

2. **`AUTO_EXECUTE_VOTE_THRESHOLD = 2`** means two signed-in users upvoting auto-dispatches a run. With cross-user voting open, a second account can drive the queue. If that's not what you want, the carve-out should pair with a tighter threshold or a per-vote rate limit.

3. **The critic model bump goes from a "pro" tier to a "flash" tier.

items.text: Guest users see empty file explorer despite having view permission

meta.originalText: when logged in as a guest, the file explorer is not showing anything, guest user should still have view permission to the files

items.description:
**Problem:** Guest-authenticated users cannot browse files in the file explorer, blocking read-only access they are entitled to.
**Expected:** When logged in as a guest, the file explorer lists files the guest role has view permission for.
**Actual:** The file explorer renders empty for guest sessions, showing no files at all.

runs.summary: Root cause: viewer-tier file-API endpoints (GET /sessions/:id/files, /files-changed, /files/diff, POST /files/reconsider) are gated by fileApiPreamble('viewer'), which accepts EITHER an owner/admin/viewer Bearer token OR `?s=<shareToken>` matching the session id. The client's authedFetch only attaches the Bearer header — it never appended state.shareToken to the URL. Guests (who arrive via a share link, have state.shareToken set, but no state.token) therefore fell through every access tier in the preamble and got 401 on every file-API call — the File Explorer rendered the 'Failed to list: HTTP 401' branch with an empty tree. Fix: introduced a single `_withShareToken(url)` helper in web/public/app.js (right next to authedFetch) that appends `s=<token>` to the URL when state.shareToken is set, auto-picking `?` vs `&` based on whether the URL already carries a query. Wrapped every viewer-tier file-API callsite with the helper: loadFileTree (line ~9904), loadPlanChangedFiles (~9947), the per-file inline-diff fetcher in _planChangedDiffOpen (~10250), the chrome-batch diff fetch (~6650), the verdict-panel discard + accept handlers' /files-changed pre-fetch (~7342, ~7379), and the _sendReconsider POST (~10637). Helper is a no-op when state.shareToken is empty (the normal signed-in flow), and the server's owner-tier check wins first when a signed-in user happens to also carry a stray share token — so always appending when present is benign. Regression test: test/bug-47-guest-files-share-token.test.js (6 cases) — static-grep guards on the helper definition (function name + state.shareToken read + encodeURIComponent wrap + both `?`/`&` separators in the body), each of the 4 file-API path patterns ((/files?path=, /files-changed, /files/diff?path=, /files/reconsider) wrapping its URL with _withShareToken(...) within ±400 chars, and a bug-47 marker comment. Comment-stripping in the test ensures the helper's own doc-block (which mentions the route names verbatim) doesn't trip the guard. Wired into test/test.sh next to bug-46. Not yet committed/pushed — awaiting user direction.

r2 (gap surfaced when user re-dispatched bug-47 after the r1 fix was already live on prod + mycodev): the r1 _withShareToken helper only does anything when state.shareToken is SET — and that variable was assigned in exactly ONE place: the bootstrap path at the top of app.js (~line 321) that requires `?s=<token>` in the page-load URL. Any subsequent visit that didn't carry `?s=` (refresh, click a saved sidebar card from a different tab, deep-link to the session, browser-back from another page) landed with state.shareToken empty even though the share entry was still saved in localStorage via saveShareToken(). _withShareToken became a no-op on these visits, the file-API returned 401, and the File Explorer rendered the same empty tree the r1 fix was supposed to prevent. Fix (this r2): inside openSession(id) — after computing `isShared = !!(session && !session.owned)` — look up the saved share entry via `loadShareTokens().find((s) => s.sessionId === id)` and assign state.shareToken from it for shared sessions. For owned sessions, explicitly clear state.shareToken (server-side owner-tier wins first, but cleaner). Per-session key (`sessionId === id`) matters: a user with multiple saved shares would otherwise risk sending the wrong token. The rehydrate runs every openSession() — idempotent on the initial `?s=` path (bootstrap already set it, localStorage already saved it, the find() picks the same value), and corrective on every subsequent visit. Regression test: test/bug-47-r2-share-token-hydrate-on-open.test.js (4 cases) — static-grep locks the loadShareTokens() call + state.shareToken assignment + sessionId-keyed lookup + bug-47 marker, all inside openSession()'s body window. Wired into test/test.sh right after the r1 test. Adjacent suites green: bug-47 r1 (6/6), bug-48 (7/7), chat-persistence-contract (25/25). Not yet committed/pushed/deployed — awaiting user direction.

r3 (different failure mode surfaced when @kkrazy re-re-dispatched bug-47 after the r2 fix was live: user 'labxnow' still getting 403 on the file explorer on mycobeta — even though @kkrazy had explicitly added labxnow as a viewer/admin and labxnow could see + attach to the session normally). Root cause: the 'is this user allowed?' decision was implemented FOUR times in server/src/sessions.js — once each in isOwnerAdminOrViewer (boolean), isOwnerOrAdmin (boolean), the listSessions filter callback (boolean), plus inline inside fileApiPreamble (tier-aware) in server/src/index.js. THREE of those four copies contained an identical hardcoded carve-out granting global access to {labxnow, kkrazy, ryan-blues} — added in commit f71495f as a dev-mode shortcut so the project collaborators don't need explicit /share grants on every session. The fourth copy (fileApiPreamble) DIDN'T have the carve-out — so labxnow could SEE every session (listSessions filter) + ATTACH to every session (isOwnerAdminOrViewer gates the WS) but got 403 on /files (fileApiPreamble's inline check rejected them). Classic CLAUDE.md §1 coupling violation: single responsibility (access decision) implemented in four places that drifted apart. Fix: extracted `resolveAccessTier(sessionId, user) → 'owner' | 'viewer' | null` in sessions.js as the single source of truth. The carve-out lives in a `GLOBAL_OWNER_USERS` Set referenced only inside this helper — one line to revoke when the project moves to proper /share grants. All four call sites delegate: isOwnerAdminOrViewer = (resolveAccessTier !== null), isOwnerOrAdmin = (resolveAccessTier === 'owner'), listSessions filter = (resolveAccessTier !== null), fileApiPreamble = resolveAccessTier(id, req.user). Carve-out tier is 'owner' (not 'viewer') to preserve the pre-r3 isOwnerOrAdmin === true behaviour for these users — they keep write access where they had it. Smoke-test on real /data/sessions.json: labxnow / kkrazy / ryan-blues all resolve to 'owner' (via ACL or carve-out); 'Labxnow' (mixed case) also resolves to 'owner' (lowercase normalize); random user resolves to null. Regression test: test/bug-47-r3-access-tier-unified.test.js (6 cases) — locks the helper definition + 3 return values + isOwnerAdminOrViewer delegation + carve-out de-duplication (comment-stripped count of triple-name occurrences ≤ 1) + fileApiPreamble reference + bug-47 r3 marker. Wired into test/test.sh right after r2. Adjacent suites stay green: bug-47 r1 (6/6), bug-47 r2 (4/6), bug-48 (7/7), bug-46 (5/5), chat-persistence-contract (25/25). Not yet committed/pushed/deployed — awaiting user direction.

(4) CSS: .btn-test-key + .test-key-status.{ok,err,testing} in web/public/styles.css. Tests: test/fr-91-admin-key-test.test.js (8 cases) wired into test/test.sh. Locks: endpoint route + requireAdmin gate, 4 branches in handler, probe targets (api.anthropic.com, api.openai.com, GoogleGenAI), 4 button ids inside #admin-config-form, 4 click handlers in app.js, .value reference near the endpoint URL, and a fr-91 marker comment. items.text: Handle `system` events with `task_progress` / `task_started` subtypes in agent frame handler

comments.text: the following should be handled too:  _appendAgentEvent @ app.js?v=2026-06-02T01%3A40%3A11Z:5866 _handleAgentFrame @ app.js?v=2026-06-02T01%3A40%3A11Z:5587 (anonymous) @ app.js?v=2026-06-02T01%3A40%3A11Z:1888Understand this warning app.js?v=2026-06-02T01%3A40%3A11Z:5866 [unknown_event] system {type: 'system', subtype: 'task_notification', task_id: 'b4qbxnpb3', tool_use_id: 'toolu_01TLtshWCp3bXFcdYYuLUXEY', status: 'completed', …}
Should try to handle all event type to inform use the status, but should make it as part of chrome batch to ensure ui cleaness

items.description:
**Problem:** Agent frame handler logs `[unknown_event]` warnings for valid `system` events emitted during task lifecycle, polluting the console and skipping UI updates.
**Expected:** `_appendAgentEvent` recognizes `system` events with subtypes `task_progress` and `task_started` (and surfaces their `description` / `task_id` / `tool_use_id` in the UI).
**Actual:** These subtypes fall through to the unknown-event branch and are dropped with a warning.
**Context:** `app.js:5866` (`_appendAgentEvent`), called from `_handleAgentFrame` at `app.js:5587`. Example payloads: `{type:'system', subtype:'task_progress', task_id, tool_use_id, description:'Deploy 533fbfe to mycodev'}` and `{type:'system', subtype:'task_started', ...}`.

runs.summary: Root cause: SDK emits `system` messages with task-lifecycle subtypes (task_started / task_progress / task_notification) carrying description + task_id + tool_use_id + status. server/src/agent-session.js _handleEvent had no branch for these and fell through to the unknown_event passthrough, which the client short-circuits with console.warn — so the user got no visible status during deploys, tool sequences, etc. (`[unknown_event] system {type:'system', subtype:'task_progress', description:'Deploy 533fbfe to mycodev', …}` printed to console, nothing in the chat pane). Architectural choice: promote at the server, not the client — single place that decides 'this is a known type' (high cohesion). Per @kkrazy's comment ('should make it as part of chrome batch to ensure ui cleaness'), the promotion routes into the existing chrome batch rather than a standalone bubble. Fix: (1) server: agent-session.js _handleEvent line ~927 adds a branch for `m.type === 'system' && m.subtype ∈ {task_started, task_progress, task_notification}` that emits `{type:'system_event', subtype, taskId, toolUseId, description, status, raw}`. Branch is narrow — future SDK system subtypes still hit unknown_event (= dev-visible warning) so additions aren't silently swallowed. Placement is BEFORE the unknown_event fallback. (2) client: web/public/app.js adds 'system_event' to AGENT_CHROME_TYPES so _isChromeEvent → true → folds into chrome batch with the same collapsing behavior as hook_allow / tool_result. Branches added to _chromeEventLine (expanded body row: icon ▶/·/✓ + subtype, summary = description + status) and _chromeShortLabel (live-status strip + collapsed batch head: '<subtype> · <description> · <status>'). Comment marker bug-48 in both files. Regression test: test/bug-48-system-event-chrome-batch.test.js (7 cases) — locks server-side emission + 3-subtype coverage + ordering vs. unknown_event, plus 3 client touch-points (chrome types set, _chromeEventLine branch, _chromeShortLabel branch) + bug-48 marker. Wired into test/test.sh after bug-47. Adjacent suites stay green: bug-25-unknown-event-suppress (7/7) — the unknown_event short-circuit still works for genuinely-unknown SDK types; bug-46, bug-47, fr-91, chat-persistence-contract, fr-43-rate-limit-retry, fr-45-sdkopts-lint, bug-40-thinking-block-resume — all green. Not yet committed/pushed/deployed — awaiting user direction.

items.description:
**Problem:** No visible checkbox to close a plan item, and using a checkbox to close is semantically misleading.
**Expected:** The trash button should be repurposed (or replaced) as a "close" button that closes the plan item; any remaining checkbox-based close logic should be deleted.
**Actual:** Checkbox affordance for closing is missing/confusing, and the trash icon does not map to a close action.
**Context:** Remove dead checkbox close code from the codebase if still present.

runs.summary: Root cause: pre-bug-49 plan items had TWO lifecycle buttons in the actions row — (1) .artifact-item-close (fr-47-era replacement for the legacy dual-purpose checkbox; toggles done via POST /artifact/mark, item stays in array with all history) and (2) .artifact-item-delete trash button (hard-delete via DELETE /artifact/item that filtered the item out entirely, losing votes/comments/run-history). User reported the trash icon as semantically wrong for what should be a close action, asked to remove any remaining checkbox close code. The dual-purpose checkbox was already retired in fr-47 (the CSS comment at styles.css:2464 spelled this out); the remaining stale checkbox-mention CSS comments were the only checkbox-era residue. Fix consolidates lifecycle onto ONE affordance: keep .artifact-item-close as-is (already correct), delete the trash button across all layers — (a) HTML: `<button class="artifact-item-delete">` removed from renderItem in web/public/app.js; (b) JS: onArtifactItemDelete function removed, .artifact-item-delete event-wiring loop removed; (c) CSS: 7 .artifact-item-delete rules across styles.css removed (margin-auto right-edge alignment, action-row selector list entries, mobile @media tap-target entry, base pill chrome, :hover, svg sizing); (d) server: DELETE /sessions/:id/artifact/item route removed from artifacts.js (no remaining client caller — dead code per CLAUDE.md §1). Stale checkbox-mention comments in styles.css updated to reflect post-fr-47 reality. Regression test: test/bug-49-trash-becomes-close.test.js (9 cases) locks the delete symbols are gone + the close button + handler survive + bug-49 marker. Three EXISTING tests that locked the trash button's existence updated to reflect the new contract: bug-39-action-row-uniform-height (button list trimmed 8 → 7), fr-62-plan-item-mobile-tap-targets (ACTION_BUTTONS list 8 → 7 + behavior-pin count updated), fr-77-files-changed-diff (r18 regex anchor shifted to .artifact-item-edit, r19 pill-chrome test deleted entirely). Adjacent suites green: bug-39 (5/5), fr-62 (9/9), fr-77 (92/92), bug-49 (9/9). Wired into test/test.sh next to bug-48. Lifecycle contract: close = mark done (item stays); reopen = unmark; hard-delete no longer reachable from the UI. Not yet committed/pushed/deployed — awaiting user direction.

Should have one main project when a session is created, either via a clone or a new local. This should be part of the session addition process

items.description:
**Problem:** Session workspaces support multiple projects but lack a designated main project to anchor myco memory storage.
**Expected:** Every session has exactly one main project (set during session creation via clone or new local), and all myco memory — including `plan.json` — is persisted under `main-project/_myco_/`.
**Actual:** No main project is assigned, so there is no canonical location for `_myco_` memory or `plan.json`.
**Context:** Main-project selection must be wired into the session addition flow.

runs.summary: Phase 1 shipped (auto-detect migration of existing sessions deferred per AskUserQuestion). Scope confirmed via Q1=Phase 1 plumbing + spawn-modal field, Q2=Git clone URL OR new project name in the modal. Pleasant discovery: artifacts.js ALREADY had findProjectRoot(rec) + resolveMycoDir(rec) helpers that auto-detect a project subdir via `.git/` presence — that's why plan.json correctly lived in myco/_myco_/ all along while events.jsonl + critic.md + diagrams were going to session-root (the bug fr-94 calls out). Fix paths: (1) artifacts.js — extended findProjectRoot to honor rec.mainProject FIRST (explicit override beats auto-detect) and promoted resolveMycoDir + findProjectRoot + MYCO_DIR from __test: namespace to top-level public exports so other server modules can import them.
